title: Suspense and pretense: The RN prepares for life after Le Pen
subhead: Pending the July 7 court decision that will determine whether Marine Le Pen can stand in elections, the far-right party must prepare for the 2027 presidential campaign without knowing whether she or Jordan Bardella will be its candidate.

'Senegal's anti-homosexuality law relies on historical and health claims that are false'
subhead: The government's repressive policy can only undermine the country's outstanding public health record, threatening to erase decades of progress, warns anthropologist Richard Powis.

title: Viktor Orban's defeat in Hungary rocks Poland's ultraconservative PiS
subhead: The party of Jaroslaw Kaczynski, which offered repeated support for what it saw as a model regime and key ally during the Hungarian election campaign, is now grappling with doubts and debating a strategic shift.

title: 'Dansons,' Céline Dion's new single, already feels outdated
subhead: The superstar's new song, written by Jean-Jacques Goldman and launched with great fanfare on Friday, seems to belong to another era. It is far from the hits that the French singer-songwriter created for the Québécois diva in the 1990s.

subhead: The spectacular production gives the new generation of dancers a chance to showcase their talents.

How Algeria tried to erase the two suicide attacks during Pope Leo's visit
subhead: Two men blew themselves up on Monday about 40 kilometers from Algiers, without causing any casualties other than themselves. Authorities sought to impose silence on the failed terrorist attack from the official narrative of the historic visit.

title: 'At that price, how can we accept?' Trump Organization's Vietnam golf project sparks protests and corruption allegations
subhead: Signed just before the 2024 US presidential election, the project has caused divisions within the government and sparked anger among local residents, who have been offered extremely low compensation for the seizure of their land.

title: Firing of respected French publisher demonstrates billionaire Bolloré's tightening grip
subhead: Olivier Nora, the long-time CEO of the Grasset publishing house, was fired on Tuesday. He was replaced with a close associate of Vincent Bolloré, who took control of France's leading publishing group Hachette Livre in 2023.
